storage_extensions.usage: mmap codes.bin; use plabel_codes[pre_row] as integer group key component instead of varlen string lookup. Only decode plabel strings for the final 200 output rows using dict_offsets + dict_data.

join_order.notes: Bitset indexed by sub_fk for O(1) filter during num scan

join_order.build: pre(filtered by stmt='IS' AND sub_fk passes fy2023)
join_order.notes: Build hash multimap from DOUBLY-filtered pre rows: stmt='IS' AND fy2023[sub_fk]. Only ~200K entries (vs 1.06M in iter_2). Key=(sub_fk, tag_code, version_code), value=plabel_code. Hash map ~6MB, fits in 44MB LLC.

data_structures.fy2023_mask: bitset(86135) — O(1) sub_fk filter, ~11KB, fits in L1
data_structures.sub_fk_to_name_id: direct_array(uint32_t, 86135) — maps sub_fk to deduplicated name_id, ~344KB
data_structures.pre_hash_map: open_addressing_hash_multimap<packed_key(sub_fk:20, tag_code:18, version_code:17 → uint64_t) → plabel_code:uint32_t>. Build from IS+fy2023 filtered pre rows. ~200K entries, 16 bytes/slot, capacity ~512K slots (40% load), total ~8MB. FITS IN 44MB LLC. Use linear probing with EMPTY_KEY sentinel. For multi-value: allow duplicate keys with consecutive probing — when inserting a duplicate key, probe to next empty slot. When looking up, continue scanning past matching keys until empty slot. Use same hashKey3 function for consistency.
data_structures.aggregation: open_addressing_hash_map<GroupKey(name_id:u32, tag_code:u32, plabel_code:u32 packed into uint64_t) → AggValue(sum:double, count:u64)>, thread-local with merge. Reserve ~32K per thread.

parallelism.notes: Morsel size 100K rows over USD range. Thread-local open-addressing agg maps, merge after scan. Pre hash map is shared read-only (built single-threaded before scan).

indexes.purpose: Get contiguous USD row range in num
Get contiguous IS row range in pre for hash map build

optimization_notes: Category A+C (plan restructuring + work elimination): Iter_1's main_scan bottleneck (56ms scan-only) is caused by ~6.2M random probes into the 275MB pre_by_adsh_tag_ver.idx (6x LLC). Iter_2 built a 32MB runtime hash map from 1.06M IS pre rows (borderline LLC) but the 10ms build cost offset the scan improvement. The key missed optimization: also apply fy2023 filter during pre hash build, reducing entries from 1.06M to ~200K (1.06M × 0.188). The resulting hash map is ~8MB (5x smaller), solidly fitting in the 44MB LLC. Build cost: ~3ms for scanning 1.06M IS pre rows sequentially and inserting only ~200K qualifying entries. During num scan, every probe hits LLC instead of main memory, eliminating the cache-miss bottleneck. Use open-addressing with packed uint64_t keys (sub_fk|tag_code|version_code) and uint32_t plabel_code values. DO NOT use the pre-built index file at all.
